steps.title: Rewrite as a quadratic
steps.content: Expand the equation and move everything to one side: $$x(kx-40)=-8$$ $$kx^2-40x+8=0.$$

steps.title: Use the discriminant shortcut
steps.content: Two distinct real solutions means the discriminant is positive. With $a=k$, $b=-40$, and $c=8$, use $$(-40)^2-4(k)(8)\gt 0.$$

steps.title: Choose the greatest integer
steps.content: The greatest integer less than $50$ is $49$. Check: $1600-32(49)=32\gt 0$, so the quadratic still has two distinct real solutions. The answer is 49.
